key_insight: A physics-informed neural network can learn transient elastodynamic wave propagation in bimaterial solids by embedding the governing elasticity equations, interface conditions, and boundary/initial conditions directly into training.
engineering_relevance: This provides a fast surrogate for high-rate impact and wave-transmission analysis in heterogeneous solids, reducing dependence on repeated finite-element simulations.
method: PINN solves axisymmetric linear elastodynamics for a steel-aluminum Split Hopkinson Pressure Bar–like specimen
Physics-based loss enforces PDE residuals plus initial, boundary, and interface conditions
ANSYS Explicit Dynamics results are used for validation and as supplementary training constraints
evidence_signal: The trained model matches finite-element predictions for transmitted/reflected waves, displacement histories, and dominant stress/strain evolution, and generalizes to unseen time instants and modified material properties.
watch_item: Generalization and robustness still depend on careful loss balancing, mesh-sensitivity checks, and the quality of FEM data used to constrain training.
paper_title: A Physics-Informed Neural Network Framework for Elastodynamic Wave Propagation in Bimaterial Systems
